data.systemPurpose: An AI API gateway and router that aggregates multiple LLM providers (OpenAI, Claude, Gemini, etc.) behind a unified interface. It handles request translation between formats, authentication, caching, rate limiting, and usage tracking, with a web dashboard for operational management.
data.architectureStyle: Layered architecture with clear separation between API handlers, business services, and infrastructure concerns. The system uses an executor framework for provider abstraction, with dedicated translators for cross-format request/response conversion. This layering enables provider-agnostic routing while maintaining format-specific endpoint compatibility.

layerMap.name: API/Transport
layerMap.purpose: HTTP routing, request parsing, and response formatting for all external-facing endpoints

layerMap.name: Executor
layerMap.purpose: Provider-agnostic request execution framework with streaming, retry, and failover logic

layerMap.name: Translation
layerMap.purpose: Cross-format request/response conversion between provider APIs (OpenAI, Claude, Gemini, Antigravity)

layerMap.name: Service/Business Logic
layerMap.purpose: Domain-specific business operations: auth, quota, cache, optimization, health monitoring

layerMap.name: Infrastructure/Persistence
layerMap.purpose: Data persistence, backup/restore, logging, and system-level operations

data.dataFlow: HTTP request → API router → format-specific handler → request validation (auth, quota, model permissions) → optional cache lookup → executor framework → provider connection selection → request translation to target format → upstream provider API → response translation → optional response caching → usage tracking and logging → client response; async: health checks, quota refresh, backup operations run via background goroutines
data.securityModel: Dual authentication system: (1) OAuth 2.0 for end-user provider connections with token refresh and singleflight deduplication, stored via CAS pattern; (2) Master admin API key for dashboard/admin API access with cryptographic randomness. Critical admin operations (password change, key regeneration, upgrade, restart, backup/restore, TLS config) are explicitly restricted from master key authentication to prevent lateral movement if the master key is compromised. Connection-scoped credential isolation.
keyDecisions: Use an executor framework (BaseExecutor/OpenAIExecutor) to abstract provider-specific execution logic and enable unified streaming, retry, and failover handling
Implement bidirectional translators between all major formats (OpenAI, Claude, Gemini, Antigravity) rather than forcing a single internal canonical format
Employ file-backed settings with in-memory caching and sync.Map for high-concurrency connection state to avoid database write contention on hot routing paths
Restrict sensitive admin operations from master key authentication as a defense-in-depth measure against key compromise
Use singleflight deduplication for OAuth token refresh to prevent thundering herd against provider token endpoints
Implement NDJSON streaming backup format with encryption support for database portability and disaster recovery
Deploy edge relay functions to serverless platforms (Vercel, Deno, Cloudflare) for distributed proxy infrastructure
